item.arguments: Use $flywheel:plan on docs/brainstorms/2026-04-18-audit-trail-requirements.md and get it ready for implementation.
special_constraints: Hand off through document-review, then let the user choose between /flywheel:deepen and /flywheel:work rather than implementing.
success_definition: Summarizes what changed during planning, what plan review found, and what execution would start with.
Makes the reviewed-plan choice between deepen and work explicit.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:plan to plan the fix for a stale cache invalidation bug in this repo. I want a technical plan, not implementation yet.
special_constraints: Bug-fix plans still need tests and verification.
success_definition: Stays in planning rather than work.
Preserves the explicit document-review boundary before execution.
Leaves a review-oriented summary and the deepen/work choice rather than jumping into execution language.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:plan to plan a retry-behavior change for failed invoice webhooks.
special_constraints: Call out rollout, observability, blast radius, or support implications.
success_definition: Treats runtime behavior as a first-class planning concern.
Mentions observability, rollout, or blast radius.
Still hands off through reviewed-plan choice rather than starting work automatically.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:plan to plan a right-sized bounded-context and ports/adapters change for a repo that currently mixes transport code with domain rules.
success_definition: Includes explicit architecture or pattern decisions with lighter and heavier options.
Carries clean-code constraints forward to work instead of leaving the boundary decision implicit.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:plan to plan a change where a shared schema preparation step must happen first, two adapter updates can follow independently, and a final reconciliation sweep finishes the work.
special_constraints: Break the work into atomic implementation units that later execution can track one-for-one.
Make serial vs parallel-ready posture explicit without drifting into execution choreography.
success_definition: Defines atomic implementation units rather than vague phases.
Makes dependencies and serial vs parallel-ready posture explicit.
Keeps the reviewed-plan handoff clear about what execution would start with first.
